originalBook: In which the remaining judgments of the Council of Carthage are examined.
title: Chapter 33

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.text: Donatulus of Capse said: "I also have always entertained this opinion, that heretics, who have gained nothing outside the Church, should be baptized when they are converted to the Church."
To this the answer is: They have, indeed, gained nothing outside the Church, but that is nothing towards salvation, not nothing towards the sacrament.
For salvation is peculiar to the good; but the sacraments are common to the good and bad alike.
